glioma_tumor: Distinguished by its infiltrative, irregular borders and aggressive growth, unlike the well-circumscribed meningioma or pituitary tumors.
Glioma tumors often invade adjacent brain tissue, setting them apart from extra-axial meningiomas.
This class shows heterogeneous signal intensity and possible necrosis, unlike the uniform appearance of benign tumors.
Gliomas can cross the midline, a feature not seen in meningioma or pituitary tumors.
The lack of a clear boundary with normal brain tissue differentiates gliomas from other brain tumors.
meningioma_tumor: Distinguished by its infiltrative, irregular borders and aggressive growth, unlike the well-circumscribed meningioma or pituitary tumors.
Glioma tumors often invade adjacent brain tissue, setting them apart from extra-axial meningiomas.
This class shows heterogeneous signal intensity and possible necrosis, unlike the uniform appearance of benign tumors.
Gliomas can cross the midline, a feature not seen in meningioma or pituitary tumors.
The lack of a clear boundary with normal brain tissue differentiates gliomas from other brain tumors.
normal_brain: Characterized by a well-defined, extra-axial mass with a dural tail, unlike the infiltrative glioma.
Meningiomas compress but do not invade brain tissue, distinguishing them from gliomas.
This class shows homogeneous enhancement and a clear boundary, unlike the irregular borders of gliomas.
Meningiomas are usually slow-growing and benign, unlike the more aggressive gliomas.
The dural attachment and extra-axial location set meningiomas apart from pituitary and glioma tumors.
pituitary_tumor: Identified by a distinct mass in the sella turcica region, unlike glioma or meningioma tumors.
Pituitary tumors are located at the base of the brain and may compress the optic chiasm, a unique feature.
This class shows a well-defined sellar or suprasellar mass, unlike the diffuse growth of gliomas.
Pituitary tumors do not invade brain parenchyma like gliomas, nor do they attach to the dura like meningiomas.
The specific location and effect on hormone production set pituitary tumors apart from other brain tumors.
